gimp-convolve.blurb: Convolve (Blur, Sharpen) using the current brush.
gimp-convolve.help: This tool convolves the specified drawable with either a sharpening or blurring kernel. The pressure parameter controls the magnitude of the operation. Like the paintbrush, this tool linearly interpolates between the specified stroke coordinates.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: pressure
args.desc: The pressure (0 <= pressure <= 100)

args.name: convolve-type
args.desc: Convolve type { CONVOLVE-BLUR (0), CONVOLVE-SHARPEN (1) }

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-convolve-default.blurb: Convolve (Blur, Sharpen) using the current brush.
gimp-convolve-default.help: This tool convolves the specified drawable with either a sharpening or blurring kernel. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-convolve' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the convolve option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: new-width
args.desc: New image width: (0 < new_width <= width) (1 <= new-width <= 524288)

args.name: new-height
args.desc: New image height: (0 < new_height <= height) (1 <= new-height <= 524288)

args.name: offx
args.desc: X offset: (0 <= offx <= (width - new_width)) (offx >= 0)

args.name: offy
args.desc: Y offset: (0 <= offy <= (height - new_height)) (offy >= 0)

gimp-debug-timer-end.help:
This procedure stops the timer started by a previous 'gimp-debug-timer-start' call, and prints and returns the elapsed time.
If there was already an active timer at the time of corresponding call to 'gimp-debug-timer-start', a dummy value is returned.

This is a debug utility procedure. It is subject to change at any point, and should not be used in production.

gimp-debug-timer-start.help:
This procedure starts a timer, measuring the elapsed time since the call. Each call to this procedure should be matched by a call to 'gimp-debug-timer-end', which returns the elapsed time.
If there is already an active timer, it is not affected by the call, however, a matching 'gimp-debug-timer-end' call is still required.

This is a debug utility procedure. It is subject to change at any point, and should not be used in production.

gimp-detach-parasite.help: This procedure detaches a global parasite from. It has no return values.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to detach.

gimp-displays-flush.blurb: Flush all internal changes to the user interface
gimp-displays-flush.help: This procedure takes no arguments and returns nothing except a success status. Its purpose is to flush all pending updates of image manipulations to the user interface. It should be called whenever appropriate.

gimp-displays-reconnect.blurb: Reconnect displays from one image to another image.
gimp-displays-reconnect.help: This procedure connects all displays of the old_image to the new_image. If the old_image has no display or new_image already has a display the reconnect is not performed and the procedure returns without success. You should rarely need to use this function.

args.name: old-image
args.desc: The old image (must have at least one display)

args.name: new-image
args.desc: The new image (must not have a display)

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: exposure
args.desc: The exposure of the strokes (0 <= exposure <= 100)

args.name: dodgeburn-type
args.desc: The type either dodge or burn { DODGE-BURN-TYPE-DODGE (0), DODGE-BURN-TYPE-BURN (1) }

args.name: dodgeburn-mode
args.desc: The mode { TRANSFER-SHADOWS (0), TRANSFER-MIDTONES (1), TRANSFER-HIGHLIGHTS (2) }

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-floating-sel-anchor.blurb: Anchor the specified floating selection to its associated drawable.
gimp-floating-sel-anchor.help: This procedure anchors the floating selection to its associated drawable. This is similar to merging with a merge type of ClipToBottomLayer. The floating selection layer is no longer valid after this operation.

args.name: floating-sel
args.desc: The floating selection

gimp-floating-sel-attach.help: This procedure attaches the layer as floating selection to the drawable.

gimp-floating-sel-remove.help: This procedure removes the floating selection completely, without any side effects. The associated drawable is then set to active.

gimp-floating-sel-to-layer.blurb: Transforms the specified floating selection into a layer.
gimp-floating-sel-to-layer.help: This procedure transforms the specified floating selection into a layer with the same offsets and extents. The composited image will look precisely the same, but the floating selection layer will no longer be clipped to the extents of the drawable it was attached to. The floating selection will become the active layer. This procedure will not work if the floating selection has a different base type from the underlying image. This might be the case if the floating selection is above an auxiliary channel or a layer mask.

args.name: floating-sel
args.desc: The floating selection

args.name: image
args.desc: The image

args.name: num-segs
args.desc: Number of points (count 1 coordinate as two points) (num-segs >= 2)

gimp-get-color-configuration.blurb: Get a serialized version of the color management configuration.
gimp-get-color-configuration.help: Returns a string that can be deserialized into a GimpColorConfig object representing the current color management configuration.

gimp-get-default-comment.blurb: Get the default image comment as specified in the Preferences.
gimp-get-default-comment.help: Returns a copy of the default image comment.

gimp-get-default-unit.blurb: Get the default unit (taken from the user's locale).
gimp-get-default-unit.help: Returns the default unit's integer ID.

gimp-get-icon-theme-dir.blurb: Get the directory of the current icon theme.
gimp-get-icon-theme-dir.help:
Returns a copy of the current icon theme dir.

Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-get-module-load-inhibit.blurb: Get the list of modules which should not be loaded.
gimp-get-module-load-inhibit.help: Returns a copy of the list of modules which should not be loaded.

gimp-get-monitor-resolution.blurb: Get the monitor resolution as specified in the Preferences.
gimp-get-monitor-resolution.help: Returns the resolution of the monitor in pixels/inch. This value is taken from the Preferences (or the windowing system if this is set in the Preferences) and there's no guarantee for the value to be reasonable.

gimp-get-parasite.help: Finds and returns the global parasite that was previously attached.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-get-parasite-list.help: Returns a list of all currently attached global parasites.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image

args.name: tattoo
args.desc: The tattoo of the required path.

gimp-get-theme-dir.blurb: Get the directory of the current GUI theme.
gimp-get-theme-dir.help:
Returns a copy of the current GUI theme dir.

Deprecated: There is no replacement for this procedure.

gimp-getpid.blurb: Returns the PID of the host GIMP process.
gimp-getpid.help: This procedure returns the process ID of the currently running GIMP.

gimp-gimprc-query.blurb: Queries the gimprc file parser for information on a specified token.
gimp-gimprc-query.help: This procedure is used to locate additional information contained in the gimprc file considered extraneous to the operation of GIMP. Plug-ins that need configuration information can expect it will be stored in the user gimprc file and can use this procedure to retrieve it. This query procedure will return the value associated with the specified token. This corresponds _only_ to entries with the format: (<token> <value>). The value must be a string. Entries not corresponding to this format will cause warnings to be issued on gimprc parsing and will not be queryable.

args.name: token
args.desc: The token to query for

gimp-gimprc-set.blurb: Sets a gimprc token to a value and saves it in the gimprc.
gimp-gimprc-set.help: This procedure is used to add or change additional information in the gimprc file that is considered extraneous to the operation of GIMP. Plug-ins that need configuration information can use this function to store it, and 'gimp-gimprc-query' to retrieve it. This will accept _only_ string values in UTF-8 encoding.

args.name: token
args.desc: The token to add or modify

args.name: value
args.desc: The value to set the token to

gimp-heal.blurb: Heal from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-heal.help: This tool heals the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. For image healing, if the sum of the src coordinates and subsequent stroke offsets exceeds the extents of the src drawable, then no paint is transferred. The healing tool is capable of transforming between any image types except RGB->Indexed.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: src-drawable
args.desc: The source drawable

args.name: src-x
args.desc: The x coordinate in the source image

args.name: src-y
args.desc: The y coordinate in the source image

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-heal-default.blurb: Heal from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-heal-default.help: This tool heals from the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-heal' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the healing option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-help.help: This procedure loads the specified help page into the helpbrowser or what ever is configured as help viewer. The help page is identified by its domain and ID: if help_domain is NULL, we use the help_domain which was registered using the 'gimp-plugin-help-register' procedure. If help_domain is NULL and no help domain was registered, the help domain of the main GIMP installation is used.

args.name: help-domain
args.desc: The help domain in which help_id is registered

args.name: help-id
args.desc: The help page's ID

gimp-message.blurb: Displays a dialog box with a message.
gimp-message.help: Displays a dialog box with a message. Useful for status or error reporting. The message must be in UTF-8 encoding.

gimp-message-get-handler.blurb: Returns the current state of where warning messages are displayed.
gimp-message-get-handler.help: This procedure returns the way g_message warnings are displayed. They can be shown in a dialog box or printed on the console where gimp was started.

gimp-message-set-handler.blurb: Controls where warning messages are displayed.
gimp-message-set-handler.help: This procedure controls how g_message warnings are displayed. They can be shown in a dialog box or printed on the console where gimp was started.

args.desc: The new handler type { MESSAGE-BOX (0), CONSOLE (1), ERROR-CONSOLE (2) }

args.desc: The name of the parasite to find

gimp-parasite-list.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-get-parasite-list' instead.
gimp-parasite-list.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-get-parasite-list' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x0
args.desc: The new x coordinate of upper-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: y0
args.desc: The new y coordinate of upper-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: x1
args.desc: The new x coordinate of upper-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: y1
args.desc: The new y coordinate of upper-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: x2
args.desc: The new x coordinate of lower-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: y2
args.desc: The new y coordinate of lower-left corner of original bounding box

args.name: x3
args.desc: The new x coordinate of lower-right corner of original bounding box

args.name: y3
args.desc: The new y coordinate of lower-right corner of original bounding box

gimp-plugin-domain-register.help: This procedure adds a textdomain to the list of domains Gimp searches for strings when translating its menu entries. There is no need to call this function for plug-ins that have their strings included in the 'gimp-std-plugins' domain as that is used by default. If the compiled message catalog is not in the standard location, you may specify an absolute path to another location. This procedure can only be called in the query function of a plug-in and it has to be called before any procedure is installed.

args.name: domain-name
args.desc: The name of the textdomain (must be unique)

args.name: domain-path
args.desc: The absolute path to the compiled message catalog (may be NULL)

gimp-plugin-enable-precision.blurb: Switches this plug-in to using the real bit depth of drawables.
gimp-plugin-enable-precision.help: Switches this plug-in to using the real bit depth of drawables. This setting can only be enabled, and not disabled again during the lifetime of the plug-in. Using 'gimp-drawable-get-buffer', 'gimp-drawable-get-shadow-buffer' or 'gimp-drawable-get-format' will automatically call this function.

gimp-plugin-get-pdb-error-handler.blurb: Retrieves the active error handler for procedure calls.
gimp-plugin-get-pdb-error-handler.help: This procedure retrieves the currently active error handler for procedure calls made by the calling plug-in. See 'gimp-plugin-set-pdb-error-handler' for details.

gimp-plugin-help-register.blurb: Register a help path for a plug-in.
gimp-plugin-help-register.help: This procedure registers user documentation for the calling plug-in with the GIMP help system. The domain_uri parameter points to the root directory where the plug-in help is installed. For each supported language there should be a file called 'gimp-help.xml' that maps the help IDs to the actual help files.

args.name: domain-name
args.desc: The XML namespace of the plug-in's help pages

args.name: domain-uri
args.desc: The root URI of the plug-in's help pages

gimp-plugin-icon-register.blurb: Register an icon for a plug-in procedure.
gimp-plugin-icon-register.help: This procedure installs an icon for the given procedure.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure for which to install the icon

args.name: icon-type
args.desc: The type of the icon { ICON-TYPE-ICON-NAME (0), ICON-TYPE-INLINE-PIXBUF (1), ICON-TYPE-IMAGE-FILE (2) }

args.name: icon-data-length
args.desc: The length of 'icon-data' (icon-data-length >= 1)

args.name: icon-data
args.desc: The procedure's icon. The format depends on the 'icon_type' parameter

gimp-plugin-menu-branch-register.help: This procedure installs a sub-menu which does not belong to any procedure. The menu-name should be the untranslated menu label. GIMP will look up the translation in the textdomain registered for the plug-in.

args.name: menu-path
args.desc: The sub-menu's menu path

args.name: menu-name
args.desc: The name of the sub-menu

gimp-plugin-menu-register.blurb: Register an additional menu path for a plug-in procedure.
gimp-plugin-menu-register.help: This procedure installs an additional menu entry for the given procedure.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure for which to install the menu path

args.name: menu-path
args.desc: The procedure's additional menu path

gimp-plugin-precision-enabled.blurb: Whether this plug-in is using the real bit depth of drawables.
gimp-plugin-precision-enabled.help: Returns whether this plug-in is using the real bit depth of drawables, which can be more than 8 bits per channel.

gimp-plugin-set-pdb-error-handler.blurb: Sets an error handler for procedure calls.
gimp-plugin-set-pdb-error-handler.help: This procedure changes the way that errors in procedure calls are handled. By default GIMP will raise an error dialog if a procedure call made by a plug-in fails. Using this procedure the plug-in can change this behavior. If the error handler is set to %GIMP_PDB_ERROR_HANDLER_PLUGIN, then the plug-in is responsible for calling 'gimp-get-pdb-error' and handling the error whenever one if its procedure calls fails. It can do this by displaying the error message or by forwarding it in its own return values.

args.name: handler
args.desc: Who is responsible for handling procedure call errors { PDB-ERROR-HANDLER-INTERNAL (0), PDB-ERROR-HANDLER-PLUGIN (1) }

gimp-plugins-query.blurb: Queries the plug-in database for its contents.
gimp-plugins-query.help: This procedure queries the contents of the plug-in database.

args.name: search-string
args.desc: If not an empty string then use this as a search pattern

gimp-procedural-db-dump.blurb: Dumps the current contents of the procedural database
gimp-procedural-db-dump.help: This procedure dumps the contents of the procedural database to the specified file. The file will contain all of the information provided for each registered procedure.

args.name: filename
args.desc: The dump filename

gimp-procedural-db-get-data.blurb: Returns data associated with the specified identifier.
gimp-procedural-db-get-data.help: This procedure returns any data which may have been associated with the specified identifier. The data is a variable length array of bytes. If no data has been associated with the identifier, an error is returned.

args.name: identifier
args.desc: The identifier associated with data

gimp-procedural-db-get-data-size.blurb: Returns size of data associated with the specified identifier.
gimp-procedural-db-get-data-size.help: This procedure returns the size of any data which may have been associated with the specified identifier. If no data has been associated with the identifier, an error is returned.

args.name: identifier
args.desc: The identifier associated with data

gimp-procedural-db-proc-arg.blurb: Queries the procedural database for information on the specified procedure's argument.
gimp-procedural-db-proc-arg.help: This procedure returns information on the specified procedure's argument. The argument type, name, and a description are retrieved.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure name

args.name: arg-num
args.desc: The argument number

gimp-procedural-db-proc-exists.blurb: Checks if the specified procedure exists in the procedural database
gimp-procedural-db-proc-exists.help: This procedure checks if the specified procedure is registered in the procedural database.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure name

gimp-procedural-db-proc-info.blurb: Queries the procedural database for information on the specified procedure.
gimp-procedural-db-proc-info.help: This procedure returns information on the specified procedure. A short blurb, detailed help, author(s), copyright information, procedure type, number of input, and number of return values are returned. For specific information on each input argument and return value, use the 'gimp-procedural-db-proc-arg' and 'gimp-procedural-db-proc-val' procedures.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure name

gimp-procedural-db-proc-val.blurb: Queries the procedural database for information on the specified procedure's return value.
gimp-procedural-db-proc-val.help: This procedure returns information on the specified procedure's return value. The return value type, name, and a description are retrieved.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The procedure name

args.name: val-num
args.desc: The return value number

gimp-procedural-db-query.blurb: Queries the procedural database for its contents using regular expression matching.
gimp-procedural-db-query.help: This procedure queries the contents of the procedural database. It is supplied with seven arguments matching procedures on { name, blurb, help, author, copyright, date, procedure type}. This is accomplished using regular expression matching. For instance, to find all procedures with "jpeg" listed in the blurb, all seven arguments can be supplied as ".*", except for the second, which can be supplied as ".*jpeg.*". There are two return arguments for this procedure. The first is the number of procedures matching the query. The second is a concatenated list of procedure names corresponding to those matching the query. If no matching entries are found, then the returned string is NULL and the number of entries is 0.

args.name: name
args.desc: The regex for procedure name

args.name: blurb
args.desc: The regex for procedure blurb

args.name: help
args.desc: The regex for procedure help

args.name: author
args.desc: The regex for procedure author

args.name: copyright
args.desc: The regex for procedure copyright

args.name: date
args.desc: The regex for procedure date

args.name: proc-type
args.desc: The regex for procedure type: { 'Internal GIMP procedure', 'GIMP Plug-in', 'GIMP Extension', 'Temporary Procedure' }

gimp-procedural-db-set-data.blurb: Associates the specified identifier with the supplied data.
gimp-procedural-db-set-data.help: This procedure associates the supplied data with the provided identifier. The data may be subsequently retrieved by a call to 'procedural-db-get-data'.

args.name: identifier
args.desc: The identifier associated with data

args.name: bytes
args.desc: The number of bytes in the data (bytes >= 1)

args.name: data
args.desc: A byte array containing data

gimp-procedural-db-temp-name.blurb: Generates a unique temporary PDB name.
gimp-procedural-db-temp-name.help: This procedure generates a temporary PDB entry name that is guaranteed to be unique.

gimp-progress-cancel.help: This function cancels the currently running progress.

args.name: progress-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this progress

gimp-progress-end.blurb: Ends the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-end.help: Ends the progress display for the current plug-in. Most plug-ins don't need to call this, they just exit when the work is done. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in.

gimp-progress-get-window-handle.blurb: Returns the native window ID of the toplevel window this plug-in's progress is displayed in.
gimp-progress-get-window-handle.help: This function returns the native window ID of the toplevel window this plug-in's progress is displayed in.

gimp-progress-init.blurb: Initializes the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-init.help: Initializes the progress bar for the current plug-in. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in.

args.name: message
args.desc: Message to use in the progress dialog

args.name: gdisplay
args.desc: GimpDisplay to update progressbar in, or -1 for a separate window

gimp-progress-install.blurb: Installs a progress callback for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-install.help: This function installs a temporary PDB procedure which will handle all progress calls made by this plug-in and any procedure it calls. Calling this function multiple times simply replaces the old progress callbacks.

args.name: progress-callback
args.desc: The callback PDB proc to call

gimp-progress-pulse.blurb: Pulses the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-pulse.help: Updates the progress bar for the current plug-in. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in. Use this function instead of 'gimp-progress-update' if you cannot tell how much progress has been made. This usually causes the the progress bar to enter "activity mode", where a block bounces back and forth.

gimp-progress-set-text.blurb: Changes the text in the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-set-text.help: This function changes the text in the progress bar for the current plug-in. Unlike 'gimp-progress-init' it does not change the displayed value.

args.name: message
args.desc: Message to use in the progress dialog

gimp-progress-uninstall.blurb: Uninstalls the progress callback for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-uninstall.help: This function uninstalls any progress callback installed with 'gimp-progress-install' before.

args.name: progress-callback
args.desc: The name of the callback registered for this progress

gimp-progress-update.blurb: Updates the progress bar for the current plug-in.
gimp-progress-update.help: Updates the progress bar for the current plug-in. It is only valid to call this procedure from a plug-in.

args.name: percentage
args.desc: Percentage of progress completed which must be between 0.0 and 1.0

gimp-quit.help: If there are unsaved images in an interactive GIMP session, the user will be asked for confirmation. If force is TRUE, the application is quit without querying the user to save any dirty images.

args.name: force
args.desc: Force GIMP to quit without asking (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: image
args.desc: The image

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: width
args.desc: The width of the rectangle (width >= 0)

args.name: height
args.desc: The height of the rectangle (height >= 0)

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: feather-radius
args.desc: Radius for feather operation (feather-radius >= 0)

gimp-register-file-handler-mime.blurb: Associates MIME types with a file handler procedure.
gimp-register-file-handler-mime.help: Registers MIME types for a file handler procedure. This allows GIMP to determine the MIME type of the file opened or saved using this procedure. It is recommended that only one MIME type is registered per file procedure; when registering more than one MIME type, GIMP will associate the first one with files opened or saved with this procedure.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to associate a MIME type with.

args.name: mime-types
args.desc: A comma-separated list of MIME types, such as "image/jpeg".

gimp-register-file-handler-priority.blurb: Sets the priority of a file handler procedure.
gimp-register-file-handler-priority.help: Sets the priority of a file handler procedure. When more than one procedure matches a given file, the procedure with the lowest priority is used; if more than one procedure has the lowest priority, it is unspecified which one of them is used. The default priority for file handler procedures is 0.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to set the priority of.

args.name: priority
args.desc: The procedure priority.

gimp-register-file-handler-raw.blurb: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling raw camera files.
gimp-register-file-handler-raw.help: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling raw digital camera files. Use this procedure only to register raw load handlers, calling it on a save handler will generate an error.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to enable raw handling for.

gimp-register-file-handler-uri.blurb: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling URIs.
gimp-register-file-handler-uri.help: Registers a file handler procedure as capable of handling URIs. This allows GIMP to call the procedure directly for all kinds of URIs, and the 'filename' traditionally passed to file procedures turns into an URI.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to enable URIs for.

gimp-register-load-handler.blurb: Registers a file load handler procedure.
gimp-register-load-handler.help: Registers a procedural database procedure to be called to load files of a particular file format.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to be used for loading

args.name: extensions
args.desc: comma separated list of extensions this handler can load (i.e. "jpg,jpeg")

args.name: prefixes
args.desc: comma separated list of prefixes this handler can load (i.e. "http:,ftp:")

gimp-register-magic-load-handler.blurb: Registers a file load handler procedure.
gimp-register-magic-load-handler.help: Registers a procedural database procedure to be called to load files of a particular file format using magic file information.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to be used for loading

args.name: extensions
args.desc: comma separated list of extensions this handler can load (i.e. "jpg,jpeg")

args.name: prefixes
args.desc: comma separated list of prefixes this handler can load (i.e. "http:,ftp:")

args.name: magics
args.desc: comma separated list of magic file information this handler can load (i.e. "0,string,GIF")

gimp-register-save-handler.blurb: Registers a file save handler procedure.
gimp-register-save-handler.help: Registers a procedural database procedure to be called to save files in a particular file format.

args.name: procedure-name
args.desc: The name of the procedure to be used for saving

args.name: extensions
args.desc: comma separated list of extensions this handler can save (i.e. "jpg,jpeg")

args.name: prefixes
args.desc: comma separated list of prefixes this handler can save (i.e. "http:,ftp:")

gimp-register-thumbnail-loader.blurb: Associates a thumbnail loader with a file load procedure.
gimp-register-thumbnail-loader.help: Some file formats allow for embedded thumbnails, other file formats contain a scalable image or provide the image data in different resolutions. A file plug-in for such a format may register a special procedure that allows GIMP to load a thumbnail preview of the image. This procedure is then associated with the standard load procedure using this function.

args.name: load-proc
args.desc: The name of the procedure the thumbnail loader with.

args.name: thumb-proc
args.desc: The name of the thumbnail load procedure.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: angle
args.desc: The angle of rotation (radians)

args.name: image
args.desc: The image

args.name: x
args.desc: x coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: y
args.desc: y coordinate of upper-left corner of rectangle

args.name: width
args.desc: The width of the rectangle (width >= 0)

args.name: height
args.desc: The height of the rectangle (height >= 0)

args.name: corner-radius-x
args.desc: The corner radius in X direction (0 <= corner-radius-x <= 524288)

args.name: corner-radius-y
args.desc: The corner radius in Y direction (0 <= corner-radius-y <= 524288)

args.name: operation
args.desc: The selection operation { CHANNEL-OP-ADD (0), CHANNEL-OP-SUBTRACT (1), CHANNEL-OP-REPLACE (2), CHANNEL-OP-INTERSECT (3) }

args.name: antialias
args.desc: Antialiasing (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: feather
args.desc: Feather option for selections (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: feather-radius-x
args.desc: Radius for feather operation in X direction (feather-radius-x >= 0)

args.name: feather-radius-y
args.desc: Radius for feather operation in Y direction (feather-radius-y >= 0)

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x0
args.desc: The new x coordinate of the upper-left corner of the scaled region

args.name: y0
args.desc: The new y coordinate of the upper-left corner of the scaled region

args.name: x1
args.desc: The new x coordinate of the lower-right corner of the scaled region

args.name: y1
args.desc: The new y coordinate of the lower-right corner of the scaled region

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: shear-type
args.desc: Type of shear { ORIENTATION-HORIZONTAL (0), ORIENTATION-VERTICAL (1) }

args.name: magnitude
args.desc: The magnitude of the shear

gimp-temp-PDB-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-procedural-db-temp-name' instead.
gimp-temp-PDB-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-procedural-db-temp-name' instead.

gimp-temp-name.help: Generates a unique filename using the temp path supplied in the user's gimprc.

args.name: extension
args.desc: The extension the file will have

args.name: image
args.desc: The image

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable: (-1 for a new text layer)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate for the left of the text bounding box

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate for the top of the text bounding box

args.name: text
args.desc: The text to generate (in UTF-8 encoding)

args.name: border
args.desc: The size of the border (border >= -1)

args.name: antialias
args.desc: Antialiasing (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: size
args.desc: The size of text in either pixels or points (size >= 0)

args.name: size-type
args.desc: The units of specified size { PIXELS (0), POINTS (1) }

args.name: foundry
args.desc: The font foundry

args.name: family
args.desc: The font family

args.name: weight
args.desc: The font weight

args.name: slant
args.desc: The font slant

args.name: set-width
args.desc: The font set-width

args.name: spacing
args.desc: The font spacing

args.name: registry
args.desc: The font registry

args.name: encoding
args.desc: The font encoding

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Whether to use interpolation (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: source-x
args.desc: X coordinate of the transformation center

args.name: source-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of the transformation center

args.name: scale-x
args.desc: Amount to scale in x direction

args.name: scale-y
args.desc: Amount to scale in y direction

args.name: angle
args.desc: The angle of rotation (radians)

args.name: dest-x
args.desc: X coordinate of where the centre goes

args.name: dest-y
args.desc: Y coordinate of where the centre goes

gimp-undo-push-group-end.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-end' instead.
gimp-undo-push-group-end.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-end' instead.

args.name: image
args.desc: The ID of the image in which to close an undo group

gimp-undo-push-group-start.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-start' instead.
gimp-undo-push-group-start.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-undo-group-start' instead.

args.name: image
args.desc: The ID of the image in which to open an undo group

gimp-version.help: This procedure returns the version number of the currently running GIMP.

gimp-xcf-load.blurb: Loads file saved in the .xcf file format
gimp-xcf-load.help: The XCF file format has been designed specifically for loading and saving tiled and layered images in GIMP. This procedure will load the specified file.

args.name: dummy-param
args.desc: Dummy parameter

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to load, in the on-disk character set and encoding

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The basename of the file, in UTF-8

gimp-xcf-save.blurb: Saves file in the .xcf file format
gimp-xcf-save.help: The XCF file format has been designed specifically for loading and saving tiled and layered images in GIMP. This procedure will save the specified image in the xcf file format.

args.name: dummy-param
args.desc: Dummy parameter

args.name: image
args.desc: Input image

args.name: drawable
args.desc: Active drawable of input image

args.name: filename
args.desc: The name of the file to save the image in, in URI format and UTF-8 encoding

args.name: raw-filename
args.desc: The basename of the file, in UTF-8

xsane.help: This function provides access to scanners and other image acquisition devices through the SANE (Scanner Access Now Easy) interface.

args.name: run-mode
args.desc: Interactive, non-interactive
